messages: My neighbors kept asking how the new job is, which turned into a mini-practice session for explaining my role simply.
It's funny how the small perks stand out: one place had amazing coffee, the other has a tiny gym I didn't expect to care about.
A friend suggested a big celebratory dinner, but I ended up wanting something low-key and familiar.
I keep promising myself I'll update my résumé this weekend, then I get distracted by laundry and a podcast.
I can't decide whether to keep an old company t-shirt as a memento or turn it into a rag — sentimental indecision.
There are a few practical tasks still hanging — updating accounts, notifying a vendor, changing an address — the usual moving checklist.
Okay, I'm ready to move forward with the actual drafts now.
Could you write a short private note I can send to the people I left? Make it appreciative and include a brief offer to support the transition.
Then please draft a concise public post for my professional circles that announces the change without getting into internal specifics.
Both should be compact — short paragraphs that I can paste directly into an email and into a public post.
Aim for a warm, forward-looking tone rather than a long list of accomplishments.
Please avoid mentioning compensation or any inside jargon from either workplace.
Keep individual names generic — refer to teams or roles rather than naming people where possible.
I prefer the private note first, then the public one, and no need for links or attachments in the drafts.
Make the language clear so someone outside of our industry would understand what I'm saying.
Thanks — please provide both drafts now, starting with the internal note.
